title: Sleep, TMD, & Technology in the Modern-Day Comprehensive Dental Practice
description:
The modern dental practice plays an expanding role in a patient's overall systemic health. This program explores the critical intersection of dentistry, sleep medicine, and orofacial pain management, designed for professionals interested in how comprehensive dental care can identify conditions like sleep-disordered breathing and TMD. The curriculum emphasizes a collaborative approach, highlighting the dental team's partnership with sleep physicians and other specialists to improve patient outcomes through early detection and integrated care, strengthening the entire healthcare team.
Participants will learn to recognize the signs of sleep apnea and occlusomuscle disorders and understand the role of advanced technologies like CBCT and 3D scanning in diagnosis. The course provides valuable insights into patient communication strategies that facilitate better health conversations. This unique conference at sea, aboard the Celebrity Xcel, offers a focused, distraction-free environment for learning and professional exchange, with continuing education credits available for multiple healthcare disciplines.
topic: Sleep, TMD, & Technology in the Modern-Day Comprehensive Dental Practice
itinerary: Barcelona, Spain | At Sea - Cruising | Valletta, Malta | At Sea - Cruising | Kusadasi (Ephesus), Turkey | Mykonos, Greece | Santorini, Greece | Athens (Piraeus), Greece

title: Managing Stress and Burnout to Achieve Wellness for Healthcare Professionals and Their Patients
description:
The increasing demands of modern healthcare, from technological burdens to challenging patient interactions, contribute to rising rates of professional burnout. This program directly addresses the physical, psychological, and behavioral symptoms of stress and burnout acknowledged by the WHO. Led by experts in mind/body medicine, this curriculum provides a comprehensive framework for understanding the root causes of professional dissatisfaction and its impact on both clinician well-being and patient care. It is designed for all healthcare professionals seeking sustainable, evidence-based strategies for a healthier career.
Participants will learn to apply mind/body techniques to reduce stress, improve empathic communication, and effectively manage difficult patient encounters. Sessions cover delivering sensitive news, integrating mindfulness into daily practice, and understanding the impact of AI on psychosocial care. This conference offers up to 14.00 continuing education credits for a variety of professions. The unique conference-at-sea format provides a focused learning environment away from daily distractions, fostering genuine rejuvenation and skill acquisition.
topic: Managing Stress and Burnout to Achieve Wellness for Healthcare Professionals and Their Patients
itinerary: Piraeus (Athens), Greece | Thira (Santorini), Greece | Souda (Chania), Greece | At Sea - Cruising | Kusadasi (Ephesus), Turkey | Rhodes, Greece | Mykonos, Greece | Piraeus (Athens), Greece

title: Prosthodontic Principles of Implant Dentistry
description:
This program offers a comprehensive overview of the prosthodontic principles guiding modern implant dentistry. Designed for the entire dental team, the curriculum fosters a collaborative approach to patient care by exploring the complete treatment arc, from initial diagnosis and planning to the execution of complex full-arch rehabilitations. By understanding the key decision-making criteria and procedural sequences, all members of the team can contribute more effectively to achieving predictable, high-quality outcomes and enhancing the patient experience.
Led by Dr. Jack Piermatti, sessions will detail treatment sequencing, occlusal scheme considerations, and strategies for avoiding common failures in implant-assisted prosthodontics. Participants will explore the nuances of immediate versus delayed implant placement and compare different retentive systems. This program provides up to 12.00 continuing education credits. The unique conference-at-sea format aboard the Celebrity Xcel provides a focused, collegial environment for learning and professional growth, away from daily distractions.
itinerary: Athens (Piraeus), Greece | Santorini, Greece | Mykonos, Greece | Kusadasi (Ephesus), Turkey | At Sea - Cruising | Valletta, Malta | At Sea - Cruising | Barcelona, Spain

title: Internal Medicine; Legal Issues in Medical & Dental Healthcare
description:
This program offers a unique dual focus on contemporary internal medicine and the critical legal aspects of healthcare. It is designed to strengthen your clinical knowledge while simultaneously equipping you with essential risk management strategies. Sessions will cover a broad range of topics relevant to today's practice, from evidence-based screening and disease management to the nuances of medical-legal challenges. This course provides a comprehensive update for any healthcare professional dedicated to delivering high-quality, ethically sound patient care and improving practice efficiency in a complex regulatory environment.
Participants will gain practical insights into topics such as cardiovascular risk management, adult immunizations, end-of-life ethics, and effective charting to prevent malpractice claims. The curriculum also addresses vital practice management issues including HIPAA updates and office billing. This conference-at-sea format provides a focused learning environment, allowing you to earn up to 16.00 continuing education credits while engaging with expert faculty and colleagues away from the demands of daily practice.
topic: Internal Medicine; Legal Issues in Medical & Dental Healthcare

title: Polishing Your Pediatric Dental Skills and Using Nutrition to Optimize Brain, Biome, & Body
description:
This program offers a unique dual focus on specialized pediatric oral health and foundational wellness through nutrition. It provides a valuable opportunity to understand the intricate connections between oral health in developing patients and systemic well-being. Sessions explore the dynamics of the pediatric dental exam, behavior management, and the profound impact of diet on the brain and microbiome. This interdisciplinary knowledge enhances your ability to appreciate the role of dental colleagues in comprehensive patient care and provides practical strategies for your own personal and professional resilience.
You will learn to recognize key aspects of pediatric growth and development, understand the link between nutrition and neuroplasticity, and explore strategies for managing patient and parent interactions. This conference aboard Royal Caribbean's Star of the Seas provides a focused learning environment free from daily distractions, allowing you to absorb clinically relevant information while earning continuing education credits. It’s an investment in a more holistic understanding of health that benefits both your patients and yourself.
topic: Polishing Your Pediatric Dental Skills and Using Nutrition to Optimize Brain, Biome, & Body
itinerary: Orlando (Port Canaveral), Florida | Coco Cay, Bahamas | At Sea - Cruising | San Juan, Puerto Rico | Philipsburg, St. Maarten | At Sea - Cruising | At Sea - Cruising | Orlando (Port Canaveral), Florida

title: Head & Neck Anatomy & Pathology for Dental & Medical Providers
description:
This program provides a comprehensive update on head and neck anatomy and pathology, designed to bridge the knowledge between medical and dental disciplines. It strengthens the collaborative approach to patient care by providing all healthcare professionals with a shared, in-depth understanding of this complex region. The curriculum focuses on the practical application of anatomical knowledge and the latest evidence in diagnosing and managing common and critical pathologies, enhancing your ability to contribute effectively to the healthcare team and improve patient outcomes.
Participants will review translational anatomy of fascial compartments, vascularization, and cranial nerves, and learn to apply systematic examination techniques for the detection of oral cancers and ulcerative diseases, including the role of HPV. This unique conference setting aboard the Pride of America provides a focused, collegial environment for learning. This program offers up to 10.00 hours of continuing education credit, fostering professional growth in a distraction-free setting.
topic: Head & Neck Anatomy & Pathology for Dental & Medical Providers

title: Family Medicine; Oral, Head and Neck Pathology
description:
This program provides a unique, interdisciplinary perspective on oral, head, and neck pathology, an area of critical importance across healthcare. Led by distinguished medical and dental faculty, the curriculum is designed to bridge the gap between systemic health and oral disease. Attendees will gain a more holistic understanding of patient presentations, enhancing their ability to contribute to comprehensive assessments and collaborative care plans. The course strengthens the entire healthcare team’s capacity to identify, understand, and manage conditions that manifest at the intersection of medicine and dentistry, ultimately improving patient outcomes.
While specific topics are under development, the sessions will focus on clinically relevant knowledge essential for modern practice. This educational program offers up to 14.00 hours of continuing education credit. The conference-at-sea format aboard the Celebrity Apex provides a focused, collegial environment, allowing you to engage deeply with the material and network with peers from various disciplines, all while removed from the distractions of daily professional life. You will return to your practice with updated knowledge and renewed perspective.

title: Sleep Medicine and Orofacial Pain; Nephrology
description:
This program explores the critical intersection of sleep medicine, pain management, and nephrology, offering a unique interdisciplinary perspective for healthcare providers. The curriculum is designed to enhance your understanding of how these distinct but often overlapping clinical areas impact patient health. By examining evidence-based strategies from both medical and dental specialists, participants will gain insights that foster a more collaborative and holistic approach to patient assessment and management, leading to improved care coordination and better outcomes across various practice settings.
Led by expert faculty in nephrology and dental sleep medicine/orofacial pain, the sessions will focus on current, clinically relevant knowledge. While the specific agenda is under development, the content will provide practical strategies applicable to your daily practice. This program offers up to 16.00 hours of continuing education credit. The 9-night cruise conference aboard the Celebrity Equinox provides a focused, collegial environment for learning, free from the distractions of daily work, allowing for meaningful professional growth.
topic: Sleep Medicine and Orofacial Pain; Nephrology
